summary: Iteration-4 requirements are complete and atomic. All six prior findings are verifiably resolved: surfaces are enumerated (R1/R2), the state space is closed via explicit states plus a catch-all (R10-R12), concurrent-activity aggregation has a concrete rule (R5/R6), the update guarantee is stated in observable push-on-event terms with a latency bound (R14), prominence is anchored to a checkable placement (R1), and the iteration-number rule matches the recorded Q7 default (R4). Every user need in the ask traces to a requirement, edge cases (out-of-order events, accessibility, cross-surface consistency, truth on page load) are covered, and each requirement is a singular, testable when/then statement. The only blemish is R2's second sentence redundantly restating liveness owned by R14/R15 - a stylistic overlap, not a coverage or testability defect.
